message:
fix(kong): detect URL-first curl DELETE commands to prevent bypass

Fix security bypass vulnerability in Kong Admin API protection patterns where
placing the URL before the -X DELETE flag would evade detection.

Vulnerability:
The original patterns required the DELETE flag to appear before the URL:
  curl -X DELETE localhost:8001/services/foo  ← BLOCKED
  curl localhost:8001/services/foo -X DELETE  ← ALLOWED (bypass!)

The second form is a common curl usage pattern where the URL is specified
first and the HTTP method flag comes later.

Fix:
Changed all 9 Kong Admin API DELETE patterns to use alternation that matches
both orderings:
  r"curl\s+.*(?:(?:-X\s+DELETE|--request\s+DELETE).*URL|URL.*(?:-X\s+DELETE|--request\s+DELETE))"

This ensures detection regardless of flag/URL ordering while maintaining
specificity (requires curl command with DELETE method and correct endpoint).

Affected patterns:
- kong-admin-delete-services
- kong-admin-delete-routes
- kong-admin-delete-plugins
- kong-admin-delete-consumers
- kong-admin-delete-upstreams
- kong-admin-delete-targets
- kong-admin-delete-certificates
- kong-admin-delete-snis
- kong-admin-delete-generic

Also:
- Add new test `blocks_url_first_ordering` with 3 assertions
- Add new test `allows_non_curl_strings_with_kong_admin_tokens` to verify
  specificity (non-curl strings with matching tokens are not blocked)
- Add Pack struct fields for RegexSet optimization compatibility
- Update doc comment to clarify both orderings are supported

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
